title: watch.rs: read-then-canonicalize still reads symlink target before security check
description:
LOCATION: src/watch.rs:65-85, watch_loop()

FINDING: The comment at line 65 claims 'Read first, THEN canonicalize to verify the path was legitimate. This eliminates the TOCTOU race: content is captured before the symlink check, so a swap between canonicalize and read cannot leak unintended files.'

This reasoning is backwards. Reading before the canonicalize check means the content of an out-of-directory symlink target is ALREADY read into memory before the check can reject it. An attacker who places a symlink in the watch directory pointing to /etc/passwd will have its content read at line 68 before the boundary check at line 82 rejects it. The check prevents the content from being published as a post, but the file's content has already been read from disk into 'content: String' in the application process.

IMPACT: An attacker with write access to the watch directory can use a symlink to read arbitrary files from the filesystem into the server process's memory. The content won't be published as a post, but there is no second-order protection against the read itself (e.g., if content were logged on error).
close_reason: Fixed: watch.rs canonicalizes before reading (same fix as r9da.2)
